302→\t\t}\n   303→\t}\n   304→\n   305→\treturn diags\n   306→}\n   307→\n   308→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\n   309→\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n   310→\t\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\n   311→\t\treturn nil\n   312→\t}\n   313→\n   314→\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\n   315→\n   316→\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\n   317→\tsMgr := ctx.State()\n   318→\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\n   319→\n   320→\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n   321→\t\tfound := false\n   322→\t\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\n   323→\t\t\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n   324→\t\t\t\tfound = true\n   325→\t\t\t\tbreak\n   326→\t\t\t}\n   327→\t\t}\n   328→\t\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\n   329→\t\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is orphaned.\n   330→\t\tif !found {\n   331→\t\t\torphans = append(orphans, res)\n   332→\t\t}\n   333→\t}\n   334→\tsMgr.Unlock()\n   335→\n   336→\treturn orphans\n   337→}\n   338→\n   339→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   340→\tvar g Graph\n   341→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   342→\n   343→\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n   344→\n   345→\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n   346→\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n   347→\t\t\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n   348→\t\t\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   349→\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n   350→\t\t\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n   351→\t\t\tg.Add(n)\n   352→\t\t}\n   353→\t}\n   354→\n   355→\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\n   356→\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\n   357→\t// instances of the module.\n   358→\t//\n   359→\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\n   360→\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\n   361→\t// be expecting check results for, below.\n   362→\n   363→\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n   364→\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n   365→\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n   366→\t\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n   367→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n   368→\t\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n   369→\t\t\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n   370→\t\t}\n   371→\t}\n   372→\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n   373→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   374→\t}\n   375→\n   376→\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n   377→\n   378→\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n   379→\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n   380→\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n   381→\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n   382→\t// visiting the checks.\n   383→\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n   384→\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n   385→\t}\n   386→\n   387→\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n   388→\n   389→\treturn &g, diags\n   390→}\n   391→\n   392→// expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\n   393→// itself in the context of a particular module instance.\n   394→//\n   395→// It has several side-effects:\n   396→//   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\n   397→//   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \"expander\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\n   398→//   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\n   399→//\n   400→// After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\n   401→// within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\n   402→// checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\n   403→// object instances for this resource instance.\n   404→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   405→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   406→\n   407→\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n   408→\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n   409→\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n   410→\n   411→\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n   412→\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n   413→\t// to work below.\n   414→\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n   415→\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n   416→\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n   417→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   418→\t}\n   419→\n   420→\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\n   421→\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\n   422→\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\n   423→\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\n   424→\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\n   425→\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n   426→\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n   427→\n   428→\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\n   429→\t// an index.\n   430→\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n   431→\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\n   432→\t// instance address has one.\n   433→\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n   434→\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n   435→\t}\n   436→\tif mustHaveIndex {\n   437→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n   438→\t}\n   439→\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\n   440→\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\n   441→\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\n   442→\n   443→\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\n   444→\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\n   445→\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\n   446→\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\n   447→\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\n   448→\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n   449→\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n   450→\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n   451→\t\treturn nil, diags\n   452→\t}\n   453→\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n   454→\n   455→\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n   456→}\n   457→\n   458→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n   459→\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   460→\n   461→\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n   462→\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n   463→\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n   464→\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n   465→\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n   466→\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n   467→\t\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n   468→\t}\n   469→\n   470→\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\n   471→\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\n   472→\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n   473→\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n   474→\n   475→\t// Start creating the steps\n   476→\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n   477→\t\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\n   478→\t\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n   479→\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\n   480→\t\t\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n   481→\t\t\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n   482→\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n   483→\t\t},\n   484→\n   485→\t\t// Add the count/for_each orphans\n   486→\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\n   487→\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\n   488→\t\t\tAddr:          addr,\n   489→\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n   490→\t\t\tState:         state,\n   491→\t\t},\n   492→\n   493→\t\t// Attach the state\n   494→\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n   495→\n   496→\t\t// Targeting\n   497→\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n   498→\n   499→\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n   500→\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n   501→\n   502→\t\t// Make sure there is a single root\n   503→\t\t&RootTransformer{},\n   504→\t}\n   505→\n   506→\t// Build the graph\n   507→\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n   508→\t\tSteps: steps,\n   509→\t\tName:  \"nodeExpandPlannableResource\",\n   510→\t}\n   511→\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n   512→\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n   513→\n   514→\treturn graph, diags\n   515→}\n   516→\n   517→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResource(ctx EvalContext, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   518→\treturn func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   519→\t\tvar m *NodePlannableResourceInstance\n   520→\n   521→\t\t// If we're in legacy import mode (the import CLI command), we only need\n   522→\t\t// to return the import node, not a plannable resource node.\n   523→\t\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n   524→\t\t\tif importTarget.LegacyAddr.Equal(a.Addr) {\n   525→\n   526→\t\t\t\t// If we're in the legacy import mode, then we should never\n   527→\t\t\t\t// see unknown imports. So, it's fine to just look at the known\n   528→\t\t\t\t// imports here.\n   529→\t\t\t\tidValue := knownImports.Get(importTarget.LegacyAddr)\n   530→\n   531→\t\t\t\treturn &graphNodeImportState{\n   532→\t\t\t\t\tAddr:             importTarget.LegacyAddr,\n   533→\t\t\t\t\tID:               idValue.AsString(),\n   534→\t\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n   535→\t\t\t\t}\n   536→\t\t\t}\n   537→\t\t}\n   538→\n   539→\t\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n   540→\t\ta.Config = n.Config\n   541→\t\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n   542→\t\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n   543→\t\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n   544→\t\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n   545→\t\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\n   546→\t\ta.Dependencies = n.dependencies\n   547→\t\ta.preDestroyRefresh = n.preDestroyRefresh\n   548→\t\ta.generateConfigPath = n.generateConfigPath\n   549→\n   550→\t\tm = &NodePlannableResourceInstance{\n   551→\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   552→\n   553→\t\t\t// By the time we're walking, we've figured out whether we need\n   554→\t\t\t// to force on CreateBeforeDestroy due to dependencies on other\n   555→\t\t\t// nodes that have it.\n   556→\t\t\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy: n.CreateBeforeDestroy(),\n   557→\t\t\tskipRefresh:              n.skipRefresh,\n   558→\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:          skipPlanChanges,\n   559→\t\t\tforceReplace:             n.forceReplace,\n   560→\t\t}\n   561→\n   562→\t\tif importID, ok := knownImports.GetOk(a.Addr); ok {\n   563→\t\t\tm.importTarget = importID\n   564→\t\t} else {\n   565→\t\t\t// We're going to check now if this resource instance *might* be\n   566→\t\t\t// targeted by one of the unknown imports. If it is, we'll set the\n   567→\t\t\t// import target to an unknown value so that the import operation\n   568→\t\t\t// will be deferred.\n   569→\t\t\tfor _, unknownImport := range unknownImports.Elems {\n   570→\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Key.MatchesInstance(a.Addr) {\n   571→\t\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Value.Has(a.Addr) {\n   572→\t\t\t\t\t\t// This means that this particular instance already\n   573→\t\t\t\t\t\t// exists within the state. `import` blocks that target\n   574→\t\t\t\t\t\t// instances that already exist are ignored by\n   575→\t\t\t\t\t\t// Terraform. This means that even if this unknown\n   576→\t\t\t\t\t\t// import does eventually resolve to this instance then\n   577→\t\t\t\t\t\t// it would be ignored anyway. So for this instance we\n   578→\t\t\t\t\t\t// won't set the import target.\n   579→\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   580→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   581→\n   582→\t\t\t\t\tm.importTarget = cty.UnknownVal(cty.String)\n   583→\t\t\t\t}\n   584→\t\t\t}\n   585→\t\t}\n   586→\n   587→\t\treturn m\n   588→\t}\n   589→}\n   590→\n   591→func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResourceOrphan(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n   592→\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n   593→\ta.Config = n.Config\n   594→\ta.ResolvedProvider = &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n   600→\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n   601→\t\tskipRefresh:                  n.skipRefresh,\n   602→\t\tskipPlanChanges: diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   608→\n   609→\tfor _, candidateAddr := range n.forceReplace {\n   610→\t\tif candidateAddr.Resource.Key == addrs.NoKey {\n   611→\t\t\tif n.Addr.Resource.Equal(candidateAddr.Resource.Resource) {\n   612→\t\t\t\tswitch {\n   613→\t\t\t\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 0:\n   614→\t\t\t\t\t// In this case there _are_ no instances to replace, 618→\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n   619→\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   620→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because this resource doesn't have any instances.\",\n   621→\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr,\n   622→\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n   623→\t\t\t\t\t))\n   624→\t\t\t\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 1:\n   625→\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n   626→\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n   627→\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n   628→\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   629→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\n\\nTo force replacement of the single declared instance, use the following option instead:\\n  -replace=%q\",\n   630→\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr, instanceAddrs[0],\n   631→\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n   632→\t\t\t\t\t))\n   633→\t\t\t\tdefault:\n   634→\t\t\t\t\tvar possibleValidOptions strings.Builder\n   635→\t\t\t\t\tfor _, addr := range instanceAddrs {\n   636→\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&possibleValidOptions, \"\\n  -replace=%q\", addr)\n   642→\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n   643→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\n\\nTo force replacement of particular 648→\t\t\t}\n   649→\t\t}\n   650→\t}\n   651→\n   652→\treturn diags\n   653→}\n   654→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01StWsmLQP5rkdya7zewXxjG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n     3→\n     4→package terraform\n     5→\n     6→import (\n     7→\t\"log\"\n     8→\n     9→\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n    10→\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n    11→\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n    12→)\n    13→\n    14→// OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphaned\n    15→// resource instances to the graph. An \"orphan\" is an instance that is present\n    16→// in the state but belongs to a resource that is no longer present in the\n    17→// configuration.\n    18→//\n    19→// This is not the transformer that deals with \"count orphans\" (instances that\n    20→// are no longer covered by a resource's \"count\" or \"for_each\" setting); that's\n    21→// handled instead by OrphanResourceCountTransformer.\n    22→type OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer struct {\n    23→\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    24→\n    25→\t// State is the global state. We require the global state to\n    26→\t// properly find module orphans at our path.\n    27→\tState *states.State\n    28→\n    29→\t// Config is the root node in the configuration tree. We'll look up\n    30→\t// the appropriate note in this tree using the path in each node.\n    31→\tConfig *configs.Config\n    32→\n    33→\t// Do not apply this transformer\n    34→\tskip bool\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    38→\tif t.skip {\n    39→\t\treturn nil\n    40→\t}\n    41→\n    42→\tif t.State == nil {\n    43→\t\t// If the entire state is nil, there can't be any orphans\n    44→\t\treturn nil\n    45→\t}\n    46→\tif t.Config == nil {\n    47→\t\t// Should never happen: we can't be doing any Terraform operations\n    48→\t\t// without at least an empty configuration.\n    49→\t\tpanic(\"OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer used without setting Config\")\n    50→\t}\n    51→\n    52→\t// Go through the modules and for each module transform in order\n    53→\t// to add the orphan.\n    54→\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\n    55→\t\tif err := t.transform(g, ms); err != nil {\n    56→\t\t\treturn err\n    57→\t\t}\n    58→\t}\n    59→\n    60→\treturn nil\n    61→}\n    62→\n    63→func (t *OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer) transform(g *Graph, ms *states.Module) error {\n    64→\tif ms == nil {\n    65→\t\treturn nil\n    66→\t}\n    67→\n    68→\tmoduleAddr := ms.Addr\n    69→\n    70→\t// Get the configuration for this module. The configuration might be\n    71→\t// nil if the module was removed from the configuration. This is okay,\n    72→\t// this just means that every resource is an orphan.\n    73→\tvar m *configs.Module\n    74→\tif c := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(moduleAddr); c != nil {\n    75→\t\tm = c.Module\n    76→\t}\n    77→\n    78→\t// An \"orphan\" is a resource that is in the state but not the configuration,\n    79→\t// so we'll walk the state resources and try to correlate each of them\n    80→\t// with a configuration block. Each orphan gets a node in the graph whose\n    81→\t// type is decided by t.Concrete.\n    82→\t//\n    83→\t// We don't handle orphans related to changes in the \"count\" and \"for_each\"\n    84→\t// pseudo-arguments here. They are handled by OrphanResourceCountTransformer.\n    85→\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\n    86→\t\tif m != nil {\n    87→\t\t\tif r := m.ResourceByAddr(rs.Addr.Resource); r != nil {\n    88→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    89→\t\t\t}\n    90→\t\t}\n    91→\n    92→\t\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n    93→\t\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n    94→\t\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n    95→\t\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n    96→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    97→\t\t\t}\n    98→\n    99→\t\t\taddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n   100→\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n   101→\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n   102→\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n   103→\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n   104→\t\t\t}\n   105→\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer: adding single-instance orphan node for %s\", addr)\n   106→\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n   107→\t\t}\n   108→\t}\n   109→\n   110→\treturn nil\n   111→}\n   112→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. It doesn't make sense to use this\n    23→\t// transformer without setting at least one of these, since that would\n    24→\t// skip everything and thus be a no-op.\n    25→\tConcreteCurrent ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n    26→\tConcreteDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n    27→\n    28→\tState *states.State\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→func (t *StateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n    32→\tif t.State == nil {\n    33→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: state is nil, so nothing to do\")\n    34→\t\treturn nil\n    35→\t}\n    36→\n    37→\tswitch {\n    38→\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil && t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n    39→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for both current and deposed instance objects\")\n    40→\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil:\n    41→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for current instance objects only\")\n    42→\tcase t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n    43→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for deposed instance objects only\")\n    44→\tdefault:\n    45→\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: pointless no-op call, creating no nodes at all\")\n    46→\t}\n    47→\n    48→\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\n    49→\t\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\n    50→\t\t\tresourceAddr := rs.Addr\n    51→\n    52→\t\t\tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\n    53→\t\t\t\taddr := resourceAddr.Instance(key)\n    54→\n    55→\t\t\t\tif obj := is.Current; obj != nil && t.ConcreteCurrent != nil {\n    56→\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n    57→\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteCurrent(abstract)\n    58→\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    59→\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s current object\", node, addr)\n    60→\t\t\t\t}\n    61→\n    62→\t\t\t\tif t.ConcreteDeposed != nil {\n    63→\t\t\t\t\tfor dk := range is.Deposed {\n    64→\t\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n    65→\t\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteDeposed(abstract, dk)\n    66→\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    67→\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s deposed object %s\", node, addr, dk)\n    68→\t\t\t\t\t}\n    69→\t\t\t\t}\n    70→\t\t\t}\n    71→\t\t}\n    72→\t}\n    73→\n    74→\treturn nil\n    75→}\n    76→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the\n    30→\t// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding\n    31→\t// instances by creating dependency edges.\n    32→\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n    33→\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n    34→\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n    35→\t\tif !ok {\n    36→\t\t\tcontinue\n    37→\t\t}\n    38→\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n    39→\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n    40→\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n    41→\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n    42→\t\t\tcontinue\n    43→\t\t}\n    44→\n    45→\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n    46→\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n    47→\t}\n    48→\n    49→\tfor _, change := range t.DeferredChanges {\n    50→\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredInstance{\n    51→\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(change.ChangeSrc.Addr),\n    52→\t\t\tReason:                       change.DeferredReason,\n    53→\t\t\tChangeSrc:                    change.ChangeSrc,\n    54→\t\t}\n    55→\n    56→\t\t// Create a special node for partial instances, that handles the\n    57→\t\t// addresses a little differently.\n    58→\t\tif change.DeferredReason == providers.DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown {\n    59→\t\t\tper := change.ChangeSrc.Addr.PartialResource()\n    60→\n    61→\t\t\t// This is a partial instance, so we need to create a partial node\n    62→\t\t\t// instead of a full instance node.\n    63→\t\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance{\n    64→\t\t\t\tnodeApplyableDeferredInstance: node,\n    65→\t\t\t\tPartialAddr:                   per,\n    66→\t\t\t}\n    67→\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n    68→\n    69→\t\t\t// Now we want to find the expansion node that would be applied for\n    70→\t\t\t// this resource, and tell it that it is performing a partial\n    71→\t\t\t// expansion.\n    72→\t\t\tfor _, v := range g.Vertices() {\n    73→\t\t\t\tif n, ok := v.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); ok {\n    74→\t\t\t\t\tif per.ConfigResource().Equal(n.Addr) {\n    75→\t\t\t\t\t\tn.PartialExpansions = append(n.PartialExpansions, per)\n    76→\t\t\t\t\t}\n    77→\t\t\t\t}\n    78→\t\t\t}\n    79→\n    80→\t\t\t// Also connect the deferred instance node to the underlying\n    81→\t\t\t// resource node to make sure any expansion happens first.\n    82→\t\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n    83→\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n    84→\t\t\t}\n    85→\n    86→\t\t\tcontinue\n    87→\t\t}\n    88→\n    89→\t\t// Otherwise, just add the normal deferred instance node.\n    90→\t\tg.Add(node)\n    91→\n    92→\t\t// Still connect the deferred instance node to the underlying resource\n    93→\t\t// node.\n    94→\t\tfor _, resourceNode := range resourceNodes.Get(node.Addr.ConfigResource()) {\n    95→\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, resourceNode))\n    96→\t\t}\n    97→\t}\n    98→\n    99→\treturn nil\n   100→}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 54→\t\tg.Add(&nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource{\n    55→\t\t\taddr:             resourceAddr,\n    56→\t\t\tconfig:           n.Config,\n    57→\t\t\tresolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n    58→\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:  n.skipPlanChanges,\n    59→\t\t})\n    60→\t}\n    61→\n    62→\tfunc() {\n    63→\n    64→\t\tss := ctx.PrevRunState()\n    65→\t\tif ss == nil {\n    66→\t\t\treturn // No As a special case we filter out\n   225→\t// any edges that are between resources instances that are in different\n   226→\t// instances of the same module, because those edges are never needed\n   227→\t// (one instance of a module cannot depend on another instance of the\n   228→\t// same module) and including them can, in complex cases, cause cycles due\n   229→\t// to unnecessary 241→module.child[0].test_object.b (destroy)\n   242→  module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   243→module.child[0].test_object.c (destroy)\n   244→module.child[1].test_object.a (destroy)\n   245→  module.child[1].test_object.b (destroy)\n   246→  module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n   247→module.child[1].test_object.b (destroy)\n   248→  module.child[1].test_object.c (destroy)\n *NodeAbstractResource) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{n.Addr.Resource}\n}\n\n// CreateBeforeDestroy returns this node's CreateBeforeDestroy status.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\tif n.forceCreateBeforeDestroy {\n\t\treturn n.forceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\tn.forceCreateBeforeDestroy = v\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\t// If we have a config then we prefer to use that.\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\t\tresult = append(result, n.DependsOn()...)\n\n\t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n\t\t\t// Should never happen, but we'll log if it does so that we can\n\t\t\t// see this easily when debugging.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\", n.Name())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.Count)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.ForEach)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\n\t\tfor _, expr := range c.TriggersReplacement {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, expr)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// ReferencesInBlock() requires a schema\n\t\tif n.Schema != nil {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Config, n.Schema)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif c.Managed != nil {\n\t\t\tif c.Managed.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Managed.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, p := range c.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\t\t\tif p.When != configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif p.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\n\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tschema := n.ProvisionerSchemas[p.Type]\n\t\t\t\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema for provisioner %q is attached to %s, so provisioner block references cannot be detected\", p.Type, n.Name())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Config, schema)\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, check := range c.Preconditions {\n\t\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, check := range c.Postconditions {\n\t\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ImportReferences() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n\t\t// legacy import won't have any config\n\t\tif importTarget.Config == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ID)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ForEach)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DependsOn() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\n\t\tfor _, traversal := range c.DependsOn {\n\t\t\tref, diags := addrs.ParseRef(traversal)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\t// We ignore this here, because this isn't a suitable place to return\n\t\t\t\t// errors. This situation should be caught and rejected during\n\t\t\t\t// validation.\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Can't parse %#v from depends_on as reference: %s\", traversal, diags.Err())\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, ref)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetProvider(p addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n\tn.ResolvedProvider = p\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n\t// Once the provider is fully resolved, we can return the known value.\n\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.ResolvedProvider, true\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have a config we prefer that above all else\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\trelAddr := n.Config.ProviderConfigAddr()\n\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\tLocalName: relAddr.LocalName,\n\t\t\tAlias:     relAddr.Alias,\n\t\t}, false\n\t}\n\n\t// See if we have a valid provider config from the state.\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\t// An address from the state must match exactly, since we must ensure\n\t\t// we refresh/destroy a resource with the same provider configuration\n\t\t// that created it.\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig, true\n\t}\n\n\t// We might have an import target that is providing a specific provider,\n\t// this is okay as we know there is nothing else potentially providing a\n\t// provider configuration.\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil && n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef != nil {\n\t\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tLocalName: n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Name,\n\t\t\t\tAlias:     n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Alias,\n\t\t\t}, false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No provider configuration found; return a default address\n\treturn addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\tProvider: n.Provider(),\n\t\tModule:   n.ModulePath(),\n\t}, false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Provider\n\t}\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig.Provider\n\t}\n\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil {\n\t\t\treturn n.importTargets[0].Config.Provider\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn addrs.ImpliedProviderForUnqualifiedType(n.Addr.Resource.ImpliedProvider())\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvisionedBy() []string {\n\t// If we have no configuration, then we have no provisioners\n\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the list of provisioners we need based on the configuration.\n\t// It is okay to have duplicates here.\n\tresult := make([]string, len(n.Config.Managed.Provisioners))\n\tfor i, p := range n.Config.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\tresult[i] = p.Type\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProvisionerSchema(name string, schema *configschema.Block) {\n\tif n.ProvisionerSchemas == nil {\n\t\tn.ProvisionerSchemas = make(map[string]*configschema.Block)\n\t}\n\tn.ProvisionerSchemas[name] = schema\n}\n\n// GraphNodeResource\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\n\treturn n.Addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeTargetable\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetTargets(targets []addrs.Targetable) {\n\tn.Targets = targets\n}\n\n// graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachDataResourceDependsOn(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.dependsOn = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachResourceConfig(c *configs.Resource, rc *configs.Removed) {\n\tn.Config = c\n\tn.RemovedConfig = rc\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceSchema impl\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachResourceSchema(schema *configschema.Block, version uint64) {\n\tn.Schema = schema\n\tn.SchemaVersion = version\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachProviderMetaConfigs impl\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProviderMetaConfigs(c map[addrs.Provider]*configs.ProviderMeta) {\n\tn.ProviderMetas = c\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDotter impl.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DotNode(name string, opts *dag.DotOpts) *dag.DotNode {\n\treturn &dag.DotNode{\n\t\tName: name,\n\t\tAttrs: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"label\": n.Name(),\n\t\t\t\"shape\": \"box\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// recordResourceData records some metadata for the resource as a whole in\n// various locations. This currently includes adding resource expansion info to\n// the instance expander, and recording the provider used in the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) recordResourceData(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\t// We'll record our expansion decision in the shared \"expander\" object\n\t// so that later operations (i.e. DynamicExpand and expression evaluation)\n\t// can refer to it. Since this node represents the abstract module, we need\n\t// to expand the module here to create all resources.\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\n\t// Allowing unknown values in count and for_each is a top-level plan option.\n\t//\n\t// If this is false then the codepaths that handle unknown values below\n\t// become unreachable, because the evaluate functions will reject unknown\n\t// values as an error.\n\tallowUnknown := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Count != nil:\n\t\tcount, countDiags := evaluateCountExpression(n.Config.Count, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(countDiags)\n\t\tif countDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif count >= 0 {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCount(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, count)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// -1 represents \"unknown\"\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCountUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\tforEach, known, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// This method takes care of all of the business logic of updating this\n\t\t// while ensuring that any existing instances are preserved, etc.\n\t\tif known {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEach(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, forEach)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEachUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\texpander.SetResourceSingle(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t}\n\n\tif addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources are not included in the state\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tstate.SetResourceProvider(addr, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceState reads the current object for a specific instance in\n// the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: reading state for %s\", addr)\n\n\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\tif src == nil {\n\t\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: no state present for %s\", addr)\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\n\t\treturn nil, {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn obj, diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceStateDeposed should be reported\", addr))\n\n\t}\n\n\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tupgradeDiags = Both of the given vertices represent specific resource instances, as\n//     opposed to unexpanded resources or any other non-resource-related object.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances belong\n//     to the same static module.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances are\n//     not equal, indicating that they belong to different instances of the\n//     same module.\n//\n// This result can be used as a way to compensate for the effects of\n// conservative analysis passes in our graph builders which make their\n// decisions based only on unexpanded addresses, often so that they can behave\n// correctly for interactions between expanded and not-yet-expanded objects.\n//\n// Callers of this helper function will typically skip adding an edge between\n// the two given nodes if this function returns true.\nfunc graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(a, b dag.Vertex) bool {\n\taRI, aOK := a.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\n\tbRI, bOK := b.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\n\tif !(aOK && bOK) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\taModInst := aRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\tbModInst := bRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\taMod := aModInst.Module()\n\tbMod := bModInst.Module()\n\tif !aMod.Equal(bMod) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn !aModInst.Equal(bModInst)\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":29,"oldLines":7,"newStart":29,"newLines":7,"lines":[" "," // ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc is a callback type used to convert an"," // abstract resource instance to a concrete one of some type.","-type ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex","+type ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc func(*NodeResourceInstanceBase) dag.Vertex"," "," // GraphNodeResourceInstance is implemented by any nodes that represent"," // a resource instance. c.Count)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, c.ForEach)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\n\t\tfor _, expr := range c.TriggersReplacement {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, expr)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// ReferencesInBlock() requires a schema\n\t\tif n.Schema != nil {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Config, n.Schema)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif c.Managed != nil {\n\t\t\tif c.Managed.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Managed.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, p := range c.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\t\t\tif p.When != configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif p.Connection != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Connection.Config, connectionBlockSupersetSchema)\n\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tschema := n.ProvisionerSchemas[p.Type]\n\t\t\t\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema for provisioner %q is attached to %s, so provisioner block references cannot be detected\", p.Type, n.Name())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, p.Config, schema)\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, check := range c.Preconditions {\n\t\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, check := range c.Postconditions {\n\t\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.Condition)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, check.ErrorMessage)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ImportReferences() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n\t\t// legacy import won't have any config\n\t\tif importTarget.Config == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trefs, _ := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ID)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, importTarget.Config.ForEach)\n\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DependsOn() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\n\t\tfor _, traversal := range c.DependsOn {\n\t\t\tref, diags := addrs.ParseRef(traversal)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\t// We ignore this here, because this isn't a suitable place to return\n\t\t\t\t// errors. This situation should be caught and rejected during\n\t\t\t\t// validation.\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Can't parse %#v from depends_on as reference: %s\", traversal, diags.Err())\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, ref)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetProvider(p addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n\tn.ResolvedProvider = p\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n\t// Once the provider is fully resolved, we can return the known value.\n\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.ResolvedProvider, true\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have a config we prefer that above all else\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\trelAddr := n.Config.ProviderConfigAddr()\n\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\tLocalName: relAddr.LocalName,\n\t\t\tAlias:     relAddr.Alias,\n\t\t}, false\n\t}\n\n\t// See if we have a valid provider config from the state.\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\t// An address from the state must match exactly, since we must ensure\n\t\t// we refresh/destroy a resource with the same provider configuration\n\t\t// that created it.\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig, true\n\t}\n\n\t// We might have an import target that is providing a specific provider,\n\t// this is okay as we know there is nothing else potentially providing a\n\t// provider configuration.\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil && n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef != nil {\n\t\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tLocalName: n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Name,\n\t\t\t\tAlias:     n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Alias,\n\t\t\t}, false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No provider configuration found; return a default address\n\treturn addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\tProvider: n.Provider(),\n\t\tModule:   n.ModulePath(),\n\t}, false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Provider\n\t}\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig.Provider\n\t}\n\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil {\n\t\t\treturn n.importTargets[0].Config.Provider\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn addrs.ImpliedProviderForUnqualifiedType(n.Addr.Resource.ImpliedProvider())\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvisionedBy() []string {\n\t// If we have no configuration, then we have no provisioners\n\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the list of provisioners we need based on the configuration.\n\t// It is okay to have duplicates here.\n\tresult := make([]string, len(n.Config.Managed.Provisioners))\n\tfor i, p := range n.Config.Managed.Provisioners {\n\t\tresult[i] = p.Type\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachProvisionerSchema(name string, schema *configschema.Block) {\n\tif n.ProvisionerSchemas == nil {\n\t\tn.ProvisionerSchemas = make(map[string]*configschema.Block)\n\t}\n\tn.ProvisionerSchemas[name] = schema\n}\n\n// GraphNodeResource\nfunc targets\n}\n\n// graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) AttachDataResourceDependsOn(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.dependsOn = deps\n}\n\n// impl.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DotNode(name string, opts *dag.DotOpts) *dag.DotNode {\n\treturn &dag.DotNode{\n\t\tName: name,\n\t\tAttrs: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"label\": n.Name(),\n\t\t\t\"shape\": \"box\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// recordResourceData records some metadata for the resource as a whole in\n// various locations. This currently includes adding resource expansion info to\n// the instance expander, and recording the provider used in the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) recordResourceData(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\t// We'll record our expansion decision in the shared \"expander\" object\n\t// so that later operations (i.e. DynamicExpand and expression evaluation)\n\t// can refer to it. Since this node represents the abstract module, we need\n\t// to expand the module here to create all resources.\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\n\t// Allowing unknown values in count and for_each is a top-level plan option.\n\t//\n\t// If this is false then the codepaths that handle unknown values below\n\t// become unreachable, because the evaluate functions will reject unknown\n\t// values as an error.\n\tallowUnknown := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Count != nil:\n\t\tcount, countDiags := evaluateCountExpression(n.Config.Count, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(countDiags)\n\t\tif countDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif count >= 0 {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCount(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, count)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// -1 represents \"unknown\"\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCountUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\tforEach, known, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// This method takes care of all of the business logic of updating this\n\t\t// while ensuring that any existing instances are preserved, etc.\n\t\tif known {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEach(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, forEach)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEachUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\texpander.SetResourceSingle(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t}\n\n\tif addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources are not included in the state\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tstate.SetResourceProvider(addr, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceState reads the current object for a specific instance in\n// the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: reading state for %s\", addr)\n\n\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\tif src == nil {\n\t\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: no state present for %s\", addr)\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\", addr))\n\t}\n\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule is an\n// annoyingly-task-specific helper function that returns true if and only if\n// the following conditions hold:\n//   - Both of the given vertices represent specific resource instances, as\n//     opposed to unexpanded resources or any other non-resource-related object.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances belong\n//     to the same static module.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances are\n//     not equal, indicating that they belong to different instances of the\n//     same module.\n//\n// This result can be used as a way to compensate for the effects of\n// conservative analysis passes in our graph builders which make their\n// decisions based only on unexpanded addresses, often so that they can behave\n// correctly for interactions between expanded and not-yet-expanded objects.\n//\n// Callers of this helper function will typically bRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\taMod := aModInst.Module()\n\tbMod := bModInst.Module()\n\tif !aMod.Equal(bMod) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn The\n\t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\n\t// request.\n\tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\n\treturn &NodeResourceInstanceBase{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\n\t\tAddr:                 addr,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) HookResourceIdentity() HookResourceIdentity {\n\treturn HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferenceable\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn []addrs.Referenceable{\n\t\taddr.Resource,\n\n\t\t// A resource instance can also be referenced by the address of its\n\t\t// containing resource, so that e.g. a reference to aws_instance.foo\n\t\t// would match both aws_instance.foo[0] and aws_instance.foo[1].\n\t\taddr.ContainingResource().Resource,\n\t}\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// If we have a configuration attached then we'll delegate to our\n\t// embedded abstract resource, which knows how to extract dependencies\n\t// from configuration. If there is no config, then the dependencies will\n\t// be connected during destroy from those stored in the state.\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n\t\t\t// We'll produce a log message about this out here so that\n\t\t\t// we can include the full instance address, since the equivalent\n\t\t\t// message in NodeAbstractResource.References cannot see it.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\", n.Name())\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have neither config nor state then we have no references.\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// StateDependencies returns the dependencies which will be saved in the state\n// for managed resources, or the most current dependencies for data resources.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) StateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource {\n\t// Managed resources prefer the stored dependencies, to avoid possible\n\t// conflicts in ordering when refactoring configuration.\n\tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\n\t\tif s.Current != nil {\n\t\t\treturn s.Current.Dependencies\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If there are no stored dependencies, this is either a newly created\n\t// managed resource, or a data source, and we can use the most recently\n\t// calculated dependencies.\n\treturn n.Dependencies\n}\n\n// GraphNodeResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachResourceState\nfunc (n NodeResourceInstanceBase.AttachResourceState for %s\", n.Addr)\n\tn.instanceState = s.Instance(n.Addr.Resource.Key)\n\tn.storedProviderConfig = s.ProviderConfig\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) ConfigProvider() addrs.AbsProviderConfig {\n\treturn n.ResolvedProvider\n}\n\n// GraphNodeOverridable\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) SetOverride(override *configs.Override) {\n\tn.override = override\n}\n\n// readDiff returns the planned change for a particular resource instance\n// object.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) readDiff(ctx EvalContext, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, error) {\n\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", addr.Resource.Resource.Type)\n\t}\n\n\tchange := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDiff: Read %s change from plan for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n\n\treturn change, nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) checkPreventDestroy(change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tif change == nil || n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tpreventDestroy := n.Config.Managed.PreventDestroy\n\n\tif (change.Action == plans.Delete || change.Action.IsReplace()) && preventDestroy {\n\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\tSummary:  \"Instance To avoid this error and continue with the plan, either disable lifecycle.prevent_destroy or reduce the scope of the plan using the -target option.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.Addr.String(),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t\tSubject: &n.Config.DeclRange,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// preApplyHook calls the pre-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) preApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"preApplyHook for %s called with nil Change\", n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tpriorState := change.Before\n\t\tplannedNewState := change.After\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), change.DeposedKey, change.Action, priorState, plannedNewState)\n\t\t}))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// postApplyHook calls the post-Apply hook\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) postApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, err error) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n\t\tvar newState cty.Value\n\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\tnewState = state.Value\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewState = cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\t\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newState, err)\n\t\t}))\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\ntype phaseState int\n\nconst (\n\tworkingState phaseState = iota\n\trefreshState\n\tprevRunState\n)\n\n//go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\n\n// writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\n// the selected resource instance.\n//\n// dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\n// NodeAbstractResourceInstance, because we don't write dependencies for\n// datasources.\n//\n// targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\n// default is the global working state.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\n\t\t// caller seems to have intended.\n\t\tpanic(\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\")\n\t}\n\treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\n}\n\n// (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\n// writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\n// one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\n// objects you are intending to write.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tlogFuncName := \"NodeResourceInstanceBase.writeResourceInstanceState\"\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlogFuncName = \"NodeResourceInstanceBase.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\"\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tvar state *states.SyncState\n\tswitch targetState {\n\tcase workingState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.State()\n\tcase refreshState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.RefreshState()\n\tcase prevRunState:\n\t\tstate = ctx.PrevRunState()\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported phaseState value %#v\", targetState))\n\t}\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\t// Should not happen, because we shouldn't ever try to write to\n\t\t// a state that isn't applicable to the current operation.\n\t\t// (We can also get in here for unit tests which are using\n\t\t// EvalContextMock but not populating PrevRunStateState with\n\t\t// a suitable state object.)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"state of type %s is not applicable to the current operation; this is a bug in Terraform\", targetState)\n\t}\n\n\t// In spite of the name, this function also handles the non-deposed case\n\t// via the writeResourceInstanceState wrapper, by setting deposedKey to\n\t// the NotDeposed value (the zero value of DeposedKey).\n\tvar write func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc)\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n\t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(absAddr, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n\t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, deposedKey, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif obj == nil || obj.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// No need to encode anything: we'll just write it directly.\n\t\twrite(nil)\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: writing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\n\t\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\n\t\t// fail to set up their world properly.\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: no resource type schema available\", absAddr)\n\t}\n\n\tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: %s\", absAddr, err)\n\t}\n\n\twrite(src)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// planDestroy returns a plain destroy diff.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) planDestroy(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n\tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\n\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type == \"\" {\n\t\tif deposedKey == \"\" {\n\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"planDestroy for %s does not have ProviderAddr set\", absAddr))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"planDestroy for %s (deposed %s) does not have ProviderAddr set\", absAddr, deposedKey))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is no state or our attributes object is null then we're already\n\t// destroyed.\n\tif currentState == nil || currentState.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// We still need to generate a NoOp change, because that allows\n\t\t// outside consumers of the plan to distinguish between us affirming\n\t\t// that we checked something and concluded no changes were needed\n\t\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\n\t\tnoop := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\tAction: plans.NoOp,\n\t\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn noop, deferred, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// If we are in a context where we forget instead of destroying, we can\n\t// just return the forget change without consulting the provider.\n\tif ctx.Forget() {\n\t\tforget, diags := n.planForget(ctx, currentState, deposedKey)\n\t\treturn forget, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedPriorVal, _ := currentState.Value.UnmarkDeep()\n\n\t// The config and new value are null to signify that this is a destroy\n\t// operation.\n\tnullVal := cty.NullVal(unmarkedPriorVal.Type())\n\n\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tvar resp providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse\n\tif n.override != nil {\n\t\t// If we have an overridden value from the test framework, that means\n\t\t// this value was created without consulting the provider previously.\n\t\t// We can just set the planned state to deleted without consulting the\n\t\t// provider.\n\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n\t\t\tPlannedState: nullVal,\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Allow the provider to check the destroy plan, and insert any\n\t\t// necessary private data.\n\t\tresp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n\t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\t\tConfig:           nullVal,\n\t\t\tPriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,\n\t\t\tProposedNewState: nullVal,\n\t\t\tPriorPrivate:     currentState.Private,\n\t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n\t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n\n\t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n\t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// We may not have a config for all destroys, but we want to reference\n\t\t// it in the diagnostics if we do.\n\t\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check that the provider returned a null value here, since that is the\n\t\t// only valid value for a destroy plan.\n\t\tif !resp.PlannedState.IsNull() && deferred == nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned a non-null destroy value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Plan is always the same for a destroy.\n\tplan = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Delete,\n\t\t\tBefore: currentState.Value,\n\t\t\tAfter:  nullVal,\n\t\t},\n\t\tPrivate:      resp.PlannedPrivate,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t}\n\n\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n}\n\n// planForget returns a Forget change.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) planForget(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\n\t// If there is no state or our attributes object is null then the resource\n\t// is already removed.\n\tif currentState == nil || currentState.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t// We still need to generate a NoOp change, because that allows\n\t\t// outside consumers of the plan to distinguish between us affirming\n\t\t// that we checked something and concluded no changes were needed\n\t\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\n\t\tnoop := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\tAction: plans.NoOp,\n\t\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn noop, nil\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedPriorVal, _ := currentState.Value.UnmarkDeep()\n\tnullVal := cty.NullVal(unmarkedPriorVal.Type())\n\n\t// Plan is always the same for a forget.\n\tplan = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr: n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\tDeposedKey:  deposedKey,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Forget,\n\t\t\tBefore: currentState.Value,\n\t\t\tAfter:  nullVal,\n\t\t},\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t}\n\n\treturn plan, diags\n}\n\n// writeChange saves a planned change for an instance object into the set of\n// global planned changes.\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) writeChange(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) error {\n\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\t// Caller sets nil to indicate that we need to remove a change from\n\t\t// the set of changes.\n\t\tchanges.RemoveResourceInstanceChange(n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif change.Addr.String() != n.Addr.String() || change.DeposedKey != deposedKey {\n\t\t// Should never happen, and indicates a bug in the caller.\n\t\tpanic(\"inconsistent address and/or deposed key in writeChange\")\n\t}\n\tif change.PrevRunAddr.Resource.Resource.Type == \"\" {\n\t\t// Should never happen, and indicates a bug in the caller.\n\t\t// (The change.Encode function actually has its own fixup to just\n\t\t// quietly make this match change.Addr in the incorrect case, but we\n\t\t// intentionally panic here in order to catch incorrect callers where\n\t\t// the stack trace will hopefully be actually useful. The tolerance\n\t\t// at the next layer down is mainly to accommodate sloppy input in\n\t\t// older tests.)\n\t\tpanic(\"unpopulated ResourceInstanceChange.PrevRunAddr in writeChange\")\n\t}\n\n\tchanges.AppendResourceInstanceChange(change)\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeChange: recorded %s change for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeChange: recorded %s change for %s deposed object %s\", change.Action, n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// refresh does a refresh for a resource\n// if the second return value is non-nil, the refresh is deferred\nfunc (n *NodeResourceInstanceBase) refresh(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deferralAllowed bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr\n\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s\", absAddr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s (deposed object %s)\", absAddr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\t// If we have no state, we don't do any refreshing\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] refresh: %s: no state, so not refreshing\", absAddr)\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Call pre-refresh hook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PreRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, state.Value)\n\t}))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Refresh!\n\tpriorVal := state.Value\n\n\t// Unmarked before sending to provider\n\tvar priorMarks []cty.PathValueMarks\n\tpriorVal, priorMarks = priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\n\tvar resp providers.ReadResourceResponse\n\tif n.override != nil {\n\t\t// If we have an override set for this resource, we don't want to talk\n\t\t// to the provider so we'll just return whatever was in state.\n\t\tresp = providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n\t\t\tNewState: priorVal,\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tresp = provider.ReadResource(providers.ReadResourceRequest{\n\t\t\tTypeName:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\t\tPriorState:   priorVal,\n\t\t\tPrivate:      state.Private,\n\t\t\tProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,\n\t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n\t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n\t\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif resp.NewState == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t// This ought not to happen in real cases since it's not possible to\n\t\t// send NilVal over the plugin RPC channel, but it can come up in\n\t\t// tests due to sloppy mocking.\n\t\tpanic(\"new state is cty.NilVal\")\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have deferred the refresh, we expect the new state not to be wholly known\n\t// and callers should be prepared to handle this.\n\tif !resp.NewState.IsWhollyKnown() && deferred == nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, \"The returned state contains unknown values\",\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, err := range resp.NewState.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tnewState := objchange.NormalizeObjectFromLegacySDK(resp.NewState, schema)\n\tif !newState.RawEquals(resp.NewState) {\n\t\t// We had to fix up this object in some way, and we still need to\n\t\t// accept any changes for compatibility, so all we can do is log a\n\t\t// warning about the change.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid new value containing null blocks for %q during refresh\\n\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr)\n\t}\n\n\tret := state.DeepCopy()\n\tret.Value = newState\n\tret.Private = resp.Private\n\n\t// We have no way to exempt provider using the legacy SDK from this check,\n\t// so we can only log inconsistencies with the updated state values.\n\t// In most cases these are not errors anyway, and represent \"drift\" from\n\t// external changes which will be handled by the subsequent plan.\n\tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, priorVal, ret.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s during refresh.\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), absAddr)\n\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n\t}\n\n\t// Call post-refresh hook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PostRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, priorVal, ret.Value)\n\t}))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn ret, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Mark the value with any prior marks from the state, and the marks from\n\t// the schema. This ensures we capture any marks from the last\n\t// configuration, as well as any marks from the schema which were not in\n\t// the prior state.
